tokens.basis: Same continuous session as R0/R1/Pages work; ~28% of session limits had been used through R1 + Pages, with R2 (spec + implementation) consuming an additional ~8% of limits. Estimated 80k tokens total. Includes one self-correction round on test failures (cornering convergence at automotive params) — see notes.md.

caveats: Same agent wrote and implemented the R2 spec; not a clean cross-tool comparison.

Self-spec defect: scenario 'Yaw rate stabilizes under constant inputs' specified 5% range/mean over the final second after 5 simulated seconds at throttle=0.5 from rest. With automotive params (m=1500, fDrive=9000, vMax=25), the longitudinal time constant m/dragCoef = 4.17 s; at 5 s vx is at ~70% of steady state and yaw rate is still climbing. The test was loosened to 30% (achievable) plus a bonus test that verifies the strict 5% IS achievable at 20 simulated seconds (~5 time constants).

Design-doc deviation: design.md said 'DRAG_DECEL = 0.5 m/s² (matches R1)' for backward compatibility with R1's coast model, but R1's constant drag has no force balance below vMax — partial throttle would never converge. Implementation switched to linear drag F_drag = dragCoef * vx (with dragCoef = fDrive/vMax = 360 N·s/m so full throttle saturates exactly at vMax). KinematicVehicle keeps R1's constant-drag model unchanged.
